Day by Day

The Itinerary

1
Saturday 20 June — Boarding
Board & Settle In, Salerno
▾
18:00Boarding
Marina d'Arechi, Salerno — Board
Board from 6pm. Meet the skipper and crew, complete the safety briefing, and stow your gear. The boat will be fully ready for departure.
19:30Overnight
🧀 Snacks & Charcuterie on Board — Marina
A relaxed first evening on deck with drinks, charcuterie and snacks as the marina comes to life around you. Early night — big week ahead. Overnight at Marina d'Arechi.
2
Sunday 21 June — Cast Off
Salerno → Cetara → Amalfi
10 NM · Est. 1h 00min sailing
▾
08:30Depart
Marina d'Arechi, Salerno — Depart
Cast off early and head west. Morning light on the Amalfi cliffs is something else entirely.
10:30~2.5 hrs
Cetara — The Anchovy Village
Italy's anchovy capital and one of the most authentic stops on the coast. Anchor in the bay and snorkel the clear shallows around the tower — good visibility over rock and posidonia grass. 🤿 Suggested ashore: dinghy in for a stroll through the village and pick up bottles of colatura di alici to take home.
16:00Overnight
🌙 Dinner on Board — Amalfi Marina
Stern-to at Marina Coppola, Amalfi. Optional: evening stroll up to the Cathedral of Sant'Andrea and aperitivo on the waterfront as the cliffs turn gold. Dinner served on board. Overnight in the marina.
3
Monday 22 June
Amalfi → Furore → Positano → Marina del Cantone
14 NM · Est. 1h 40min total sailing time
▾
09:30~45 min
Fiordo di Furore — Swim Stop
One of the most dramatic spots on the entire coast — a narrow fjord sliced vertically into the cliffs, invisible until you're almost upon it. Anchor just off the entrance and swim in through the gorge to the tiny pebble beach inside. 🤿 The rocky walls and clear water make for good snorkelling too. Go early — it gets crowded by late morning.
11:00Afternoon
Positano — Anchor
Anchor off the beach. Swim off the boat and relax through the afternoon. Suggested ashore: dinghy in and explore Via dei Mulini's boutiques and the narrow lanes.

⚑ Optional: Path of the Gods — Sentiero degli Dei
One of the great walks of southern Italy, tracing a ridge 500m above the sea with unbroken views along the entire Amalfi Coast. Difficulty: Easy to Moderate — the classic direction (Bomerano → Nocelle) runs almost entirely downhill on well-maintained stone paths. No scrambling or technical sections. Total distance 7.5km, 2.5–3 hrs walking. Cumulative descent ≈650m.

Directions from the boat: Dinghy ashore by 08:00. Taxi from Positano waterfront to Bomerano / Agerola (≈30 min, ≈€35–45 — arrange the night before if possible). Walk east to west along the waymarked trail, with the sea always in front of you. The path passes through ancient terraces and abandoned farmhouses before arriving at the hamlet of Nocelle. From Nocelle, descend the 1,800 stone steps into Positano (≈45 min, steep but clearly signed) and dinghy back to the boat for a well-earned swim. Aim to start walking no later than 09:00 — late June heat on the open ridge can be intense after midday.

What to bring: Trail shoes or sturdy trainers · 2L water per person · sun hat & sunscreen · light snack · cash for the taxi.
16:30Overnight
🍽️ Dinner Ashore — Marina del Cantone, Nerano
Anchor in this sheltered turquoise bay inside the Punta Campanella Marine Reserve. Dinner ashore tonight — this is the birthplace of spaghetti alla Nerano (zucchini, aged provola, basil) and it must be eaten here. Maria Grazia is the better call for your group — it's the more traditional, affordable option and arguably more authentic. For 10 people, call ahead the moment you know you're heading there — this is a small village and tables for large groups book out fast. Overnight on anchor — one of the finest anchorages on the coast.
4
Tuesday 23 June
Capri — Blue Grotto & Faraglioni
12 NM · Est. 1h 20min total sailing time
▾
08:30~90 min
Blue Grotto
Arrive before 9am — queues build fast in late June and the grotto closes if westerly swell exceeds ~50cm. Transfer to the mandatory small rowboats from your catamaran. Worth every minute inside.
11:00Afternoon
Marina Grande, Capri — Faraglioni Snorkel
Anchor off Marina Grande. Motor the dinghy around to the Faraglioni rock stacks and snorkel the base of the arches — excellent marine life shelters in the rock. 🤿 Crystal clear. Suggested ashore: funicular up to Capri town and the Piazzetta, Gardens of Augustus for the views.
20:00Overnight
🌙 Dinner on Board — Marina Grande, Capri
Dinner served on board with the lights of Capri town above you. Overnight on anchor off Marina Grande.
5
Wednesday 24 June
Capri → Procida
16 NM · Est. 1h 50min total sailing time
▾
09:00Morning
Morning Swim & Snorkel off Capri
🤿 One last snorkel in Capri's famous waters — the seabed around the anchor is excellent — before heading north across the Gulf.
12:00~3 hrs
🍽️ Dinner on Board — Procida
The most authentic and unhurried island in the Gulf of Naples. Berth in the marina. Dinner on board or ashore — dine on board OR Procida has plenty of honest, unpretentious trattorias along the Marina Corricella waterfront. Both are a great option. Overnight in Procida marina.
6
Thursday 25 June
Procida → Ischia → Li Galli → Positano
18 NM · Est. 2h 30min total sailing time
▾
09:00~1 hr
Ischia — Baia di San Montano
Short hop to Ischia for a mid-morning swim stop in the stunning turquoise shallows of Baia di San Montano. 🤿 Good snorkelling over the sandy bottom and volcanic rock outcrops. Optional: visit the Poseidon or Negombo thermal gardens, or the medieval Aragonese Castle.
12:30~1 hr
Li Galli — The Sirens' Islands
A small private archipelago known in antiquity as home to Homer's Sirens, once owned by Rudolf Nureyev. Anchor off for a swim and snorkel in extraordinary clear water. 🤿 Rocky seabed with excellent visibility — often possible to see the bottom in 15m+.
15:30Overnight
🍽️ Dinner Ashore — Positano
Anchor off Positano beach for the final night. Dinner ashore tonight — dinghy in and enjoy the evening. Next2 or Il Ritrovo are both genuinely good restaurants but fall into the expensive/dressy category — exactly what you want to avoid. Suggested swap: Go to Ristorante Buca di Bacco (casual, right on the beach, no dress code) or simply grab a table at one of the simpler trattorie along Via Pasitea. For 10 people in Positano in late June, booking is essential — the village is tiny and peak season is brutal. Book at least a week ahead. Overnight on anchor.
7
Friday 26 June — Homeward
Positano → Vietri sul Mare → Salerno
16 NM · Est. 2h 00min total sailing time
▾
10:00~1 hr
Vietri sul Mare — Ceramics & Swim Stop
Anchor off the colourful town of Vietri — Italy's ceramics capital, its harbour front stacked with vivid hand-painted tiles and pottery. 🤿 Good snorkelling off the rocky shoreline below the old town. Suggested ashore: dinghy in and browse the workshops along Via Madonna degli Angeli — a perfect spot to pick up something to take home.
17:00Arrive
Marina d'Arechi, Salerno — Return
Back at base by late afternoon. Bags packed and ready for the morning.
20:00Overnight
🌙 Dinner on Board — Final Night, Salerno
Dinner served on board at the marina. Seven nights, two coastlines, three islands, one extraordinary sea. Overnight aboard.
8
Saturday 27 June — Disembark
Farewell, Salerno
▾
08:00Morning
Final Breakfast on Deck
A last coffee and breakfast on board with the marina quiet around you. Collect your bags.
10:00Disembark
Marina d'Arechi, Salerno — Check Out
Disembark by 10am. Final handover with the skipper. Until next time.
Aboard the Lagoon 60

Life on Board

What happens if the weather is bad?
Although the weather tends to be very nice in the summer seasons, you never know. Our team knows the sailing areas perfectly and will be able to recommend the best ports, bays and places to make your stay safe and enjoyable — even when the sun isn't shining.
What should I bring with me?
Everything you need for a summer vacation — swimwear and flip-flops, cover-ups, sarongs and bermuda shorts. Don't forget sunscreen and ecological soaps (the fish will thank you). Pack in a soft bag that folds away easily for storage below. Rigid suitcases aren't welcome on board, but we can store them empty at our luggage facility if needed.
Captain's Notes

Late June Tips

🌊
Blue Grotto
Arrive before 9am. The grotto closes entirely when westerly swell exceeds ~50cm — have a backup plan. Queues can be 2hrs+ by mid-morning in peak season.
⚓
Anchorages
Late June is high season. Arrive at popular bays (Positano, Marina del Cantone) before noon to secure a good spot — they fill up fast.
🌬️
Wind Pattern
Expect light mornings with afternoon Maestrale (NW wind) building to 10–15kts. Plan longer passages for the morning. The return leg east can be a lovely reach.
🛒
Provisioning
Stock up thoroughly in Salerno before departure. Prices on Capri and Ischia in particular are significantly higher than the mainland.
🐟
Must Eat
Spaghetti alla Nerano in Nerano. Fresh-caught seafood on Procida. Hand-painted ceramics in Vietri sul Mare. Don't miss the thermal springs on Ischia's Maronti beach.
🏊
Marine Reserve
Punta Campanella Marine Reserve (near Nerano) requires anchoring in designated areas. The visibility is exceptional — bring snorkelling gear.
Getting to Salerno

How to Get There

🚌
By Bus
From Naples airport (aeroportodinapoli.it) and from the city of Naples, reach Salerno via SITA bus (sitasudtrasporti.it), then continue to Marina di Arechi by private transfer or taxi.
🚆
By Train
Reach Salerno from all major Italian cities with Trenitalia (trenitalia.it) or Italo (italotreno.it), then continue to Marina di Arechi by private transfer or taxi.
🚗
By Car
Heading north: A1 Napoli–Salerno — after the Salerno centre exit take the ring road and exit at «Mariconda» A30 Caserta–Salerno. Proceed to «Salerno–Fratte», then head south and exit at «Mariconda».

Heading south: A3 Salerno–Reggio Calabria — take the «Pontecagnano» exit and follow signs for «Fratte–Tangenziale», then exit at «Zona Industriale» or «Mariconda».
⚓
Parking at Marina di Arechi
Guest Parking is available directly at the marina. Leave your car at the port and step straight aboard.
